page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-240GB-1xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for memory-intensive and accelerated computing workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8462Y+ processor with 12 vCPUs consisting of 12 physical cores and 1 thread per core, running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 240.0 GB of system memory, providing 20.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage, requiring external storage configurations.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific data processing and machine learning tasks. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the instance achieved top-tier performance with 10515.12 tokens per second for prompt processing and 547.27 tokens per second for text generation. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also reached top-tier levels at 35.51 GB/sec and 15.51 GB/sec respectively. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks, such as stress-ng and PassMark, generally align with average tier expectations, with a PassMark CPU score of 32078.76 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 26217.73.

The instance presents specific architectural tradeoffs. While it offers a high-capacity NVIDIA H100 GPU and a substantial 240.0 GB memory footprint, the CPU allocation is shared, which may introduce performance variability under sustained CPU-bound workloads. Additionally, the complete absence of local storage requires network-attached storage configurations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high ratio of system memory to vCPUs combined with a top-tier Hopper GPU makes it a specialized resource density option for workloads that prioritize GPU acceleration and large memory capacity over dedicated CPU cores. It is suitable for LLM inference, machine learning model training, data science jobs, and memory-heavy parallel processing.

Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 20.0 GB per core but lacks local storage. In benchmark testing, it demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference, achieving 10515.12 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks fall within average ranges, reflecting its shared CPU allocation tradeoff. This server is configured for GPU-accelerated workloads, machine learning inference, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining an NVIDIA H100 GPU with a high-capacity 240 GB memory footprint for accelerated computing workloads.
